images.alt:
Back in the day the potential of the internet was being smothered by the dominance of Microsoft's browser, Internet Explorer. So those of us of techie orientation would encourage friends and family to install Firefox or Chrome instead, or would even just do it for them surreptitiously because most people don't really care what their web browser is as long as it works. Nowadays you don't hear about Internet Explorer at all anymore, and we're all better off for it.

It's time to do that again, but for Google. Google has announced that the search product they made their name on will no longer provide a list of links to other websites at all, but an "AI-driven experience" replacing references from across the web with a kind of digest of training data sprinkled with confident falsehoods and no way to tell where the information was sourced from. They've announced, essentially, that they are at war with the very web they built their success on and that they trained their "AI" models on. It is hard to overstate the damage this would do to the production and distribution of human knowledge. I think this is far more important than choice of browser ever was.

For about a year I have been a happy customer of Kagi, a paid alternative that has some "AI" features but you can turn them off. The search results are high quality and the product respects your choices when you decide you don't want something appearing in your results. But I am a niche user who makes dozens of searches per day and so the price is well worth it. Most people will want to stick to free products. For that I can recommend DuckDuckGo (again, turn off their "AI" feature, they will respect your choice, or use noai.duckduckgo.com). I've also heard mojeek.com as an alternative, but I haven't used it myself.

At the very least, on your phones, tablets, and computers, change your default search engine to anything but Google. DuckDuckGo is often available as a choice in the settings without having to do anything extra.

Many like myself started un-Googling their lives years ago when the earliest signs were appearing that this company would become a detriment to the world. But it's not enough. People for whom technology is not a primary pursuit are Google's real bread and butter, and they won't feel it without such people walking away. I am happy to help.

Back in the day the potential of the internet was being smothered by the dominance of Microsoft's browser, Internet Explorer. So those of us of techie orientation would encourage friends and family to install Firefox or Chrome instead, or would even just do it for them surreptitiously because most people don't really care what their web browser is as long as it works. Nowadays you don't hear about Internet Explorer at all anymore, and we're all better off for it.

It's time to do that again, but for Google. Google has announced that the search product they made their name on will no longer provide a list of links to other websites at all, but an "AI-driven experience" replacing references from across the web with a kind of digest of training data sprinkled with confident falsehoods and no way to tell where the information was sourced from. They've announced, essentially, that they are at war with the very web they built their success on and that they trained their "AI" models on. It is hard to overstate the damage this would do to the production and distribution of human knowledge. I think this is far more important than choice of browser ever was.

For about a year I have been a happy customer of Kagi, a paid alternative that has some "AI" features but you can turn them off. The search results are high quality and the product respects your choices when you decide you don't want something appearing in your results. But I am a niche user who makes dozens of searches per day and so the price is well worth it. Most people will want to stick to free products. For that I can recommend DuckDuckGo (again, turn off their "AI" feature, they will respect your choice, or use noai.duckduckgo.com). I've also heard mojeek.com as an alternative, but I haven't used it myself.

At the very least, on your phones, tablets, and computers, change your default search engine to anything but Google. DuckDuckGo is often available as a choice in the settings without having to do anything extra.

Many like myself started un-Googling their lives years ago when the earliest signs were appearing that this company would become a detriment to the world. But it's not enough. People for whom technology is not a primary pursuit are Google's real bread and butter, and they won't feel it without such people walking away. I am happy to help.

Der Manga »World's End Harem: Fantasia (Shūmatsu no Harem Fantasia)« von LINK & SAVAN ist am 26. April 2026 auf der Webplattform »Shounen Jump+« von Shueisha zu Ende gegangen. Die Reihe startete in Japan am 19. April 2018 auf »Shounen Jump+« und umfasst bislang 16 Bände. Seven Seas Entertainment veröffentlicht das Werk auf Englisch, während hierzulande bisher das Werk nicht auf Deutsch erhältlich ist.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Das Königreich Nargala steht am Rand des Untergangs. Dunkelheit und Verfall haben die Welt erfasst, während Kronerbe Arc noch immer nach einer Macht sucht, mit der er sein Schicksal wenden kann. Als die geheimnisvolle Dunkelelfe Lati vor ihm erscheint, bietet sie ihm genau diese Kraft an. Doch ihr Angebot führt ihn auf einen gefährlichen Weg, auf dem Begierde, Macht und der drohende Zusammenbruch der Welt untrennbar miteinander verbunden sind.

Mit einem Teaser gab der japanische  Verlag Kadokawa bekannt, dass der Manga »The Eccentric Doctor of the Moon Flower Kingdom (Gekkakoku Kīden)« von Tōru Himuka eine Anime-Adaption erhält. 
Dieser entsteht demnach bei Studio Elle unter der Regie von Kōichirō Kuroda. Die Serieskomposition übernimmt Aki Mizuki (Risa Mizuno), während das Charakterdesign von Akira Ono stammt. Die Soundregie liegt bei Hozumi Gōda und die Musik komponiert Shiho Terada, während HIGHWAY STAR für die Musikproduktion verantwortlich ist. Weitere Informationen zum Starttermin sind bislang nicht bekannt.

In Japan erscheint die Reihe seit Januar 2018 im »Asuka«-Magazin von Kadokawa und umfasst mit dem heute veröffentlichten neuesten Band bislang 15 Bände. Seven Seas Entertainment veröffentlicht das Werk auf Englisch (13. Bände), während Altraverse die Reihe hierzulande unter »Koyo: Die exzentrische Ärztin des Mondblumenreiches« auf Deutsch verlegt (9. Bände).

📜 Beschreibung 》
Im Reich der Mondblume gilt die heilende Badekunst als bewährtes Mittel gegen Krankheiten und Verletzungen. Doch als Kronprinz Keiun während einer Inspektionsreise Zeuge wird, wie die junge Ärztin Koyō eine erstaunliche Wundnaht durchführt, gerät sein Blick auf die Medizin des Landes ins Wanken. Zwischen alten Überzeugungen, politischen Intrigen und tief verwurzelten Traditionen stellen sich die beiden den Problemen ihres Reiches.

Steven Rosenbaum has decided that the real villain behind the bogus quotes in his book is a chatbot. Earlier this week, The New York Times reported that The Future of Truth, Rosenbaum's much-discussed book about how AI shapes reality, contains more than half a dozen fake or misattributed quotes.
Rosenbaum pinned some of them on his use of AI. He claimed responsibility for the errors and said he was investigating what went wrong. By the time I spoke with him on Thursday, though, he was pointing his finger elsewhere. ChatGPT "fucked up the book," Rosenbaum said.

Rosenbaum pinned some of them on his use of AI. He claimed responsibility for the errors and said he was investigating what went wrong. By the time I spoke with him on Thursday, though, he was pointing his finger elsewhere. ChatGPT "fucked up the book," Rosenbaum said.

contrition, or they can attempt to cover up their mistakes and demonstrate a
weakness of character unsuited to the legal profession. If they choose the former
